summary: Tested 3 workout-detail changes: (1) exercise ordering via _slot_priority, (2) drag-handle-only trigger, (3) 'How Hard to Train' removed. All 11 backend tests passed, all frontend UI checks passed.

react_native_issues.issue: No data-testid attributes on any exercise cards, drag handles, or coaching rows — automated selection relies entirely on text/screenshot inspection. Not a functional bug but makes future regression testing difficult.

action_items: Consider adding testID attributes to exercise cards (e.g. testID='exercise-card-0'), drag handles (testID='drag-handle-0'), and coaching rows (testID='coaching-why-this-split', 'coaching-progression', 'coaching-weekly-blueprint') for future automated testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 iteration-16 changes confirmed working. Exercise ordering: _slot_priority returns primary_compound=0, secondary_compound=1, accessories=2-4, ARM_ISO(bicep_curl/tricep_push)=5, CORE_PAT(core_stability/core_flexion/carry)=6, conditioning=99 — verified via unit tests on EliteCoachingEngine.build_blueprint() and via live API. Drag handle renders as View (decorative) on web, Pressable with onPressIn={drag} on native — visible as three-line icon on left of each card. Card tap (not handle) expands to show INSTRUCTIONS/EQUIPMENT/MUSCLES — verified. 'How Hard to Train' section confirmed absent from coaching breakdown. Coaching breakdown correctly shows WHY THIS SPLIT, PROGRESSION METHOD, DELOAD PROTOCOL, WEEKLY BLUEPRINT.

drag_handle_only_trigger.note: Three-line drag handle icon visible on left of every exercise card. Card tap (center/right) expands card correctly. On web, handle is View (decorative) — drag is native-only.
